pages.title: PillarPanel
pages.description:
Renders the Pillar help panel at a custom location in the DOM.
Use this when you want to control where the panel is rendered instead of
having it automatically appended to document.body.

**Important**: When using this component, set `panel.container: 'manual'` in your
PillarProvider config to prevent automatic mounting.

pages.title: usePillarTool()
pages.description:
Register one or more Pillar tools with co-located metadata and handlers.

The tools are registered when the component mounts and automatically
unregistered when it unmounts. The `execute` functions always capture
the latest React state and props via refs, so you don't need to worry
about stale closures.

pages.title: usePillar()
pages.description: Composable to access the Pillar SDK instance and state
pages.signature: function usePillar<TActions extends SyncActionDefinitions | ActionDefinitions = SyncActionDefinitions>(): TypedUsePillarResult<TActions>

properties.name: onTask
properties.type:
<TName extends ActionNames<TActions>>(
    taskName: TName,
    handler: (data: ActionDataType<TActions, TName>) => void
  ) => () => void

examples: Basic usage (untyped) ```vue

<script setup lang="ts">
import { usePillar } from '@pillar-ai/vue';
import { actions } from '@/lib/pillar/actions';
import { onMounted, onUnmounted } from 'vue';

const { pillar, onTask } = usePillar<typeof actions>();

let unsub: (() => void) | undefined;

onMounted(() => {
// TypeScript knows data has { type, url, name }
unsub = onTask('add_new_source', (data) => {
console.log(data.url); // ✓ Typed!
});
});

onUnmounted(() => {
unsub?.();
});
</script>

const { isOpen, toggle, openChat } = useHelpPanel();
</script>

    <button

pages.title: Panel
pages.description: Open, close, and control the help panel.

methods.name: open
methods.description: Open the help panel

parameters.name: options

methods.name: close
methods.description: Close the help panel

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Toggle the help panel

methods.name: navigate
methods.signature: navigate(view: string, params?: Record<string, string>): void
methods.description: Navigate to a specific view in the panel

properties.name: isPanelOpen
properties.description: Whether the panel is currently open

types.name: PanelConfig

properties.name: position
properties.description: Which side of the screen the panel appears on.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: Panel mode: 'overlay' slides over content, 'push' shifts content aside.

properties.name: container
properties.description:
Custom mount point for the panel.
- CSS selector string (e.g., '#pillar-panel')
- HTMLElement reference
- 'manual' for React component-based mounting
- undefined (default) mounts to document.body

properties.name: useShadowDOM
properties.description:
Whether to use Shadow DOM for style isolation.
- false (default): Panel renders in regular DOM, inherits host app CSS.
  Custom cards can use the host app's design system (Tailwind, etc.)
- true: Panel renders in Shadow DOM, fully isolated from host CSS.
  Use this if you need style isolation on third-party sites.

properties.name: hoverBreakpoint
properties.description:
Viewport width below which the panel switches from 'push' mode to 'hover' mode.
In hover mode, the panel floats over content instead of pushing it aside.
- number: The breakpoint in pixels (default: 1200)
- false: Disable responsive behavior, always use push mode

properties.name: hoverBackdrop
properties.description:
Whether to show a backdrop overlay when the panel is in hover mode.
Only applies when viewport is below hoverBreakpoint.

properties.name: fullWidthBreakpoint
properties.description: Viewport width below which the panel takes full screen width.

properties.name: initialOpen
properties.description:
Whether to open the panel automatically on initialization.
Takes priority over localStorage persisted state.

properties.name: resizable
properties.description:
Whether the panel can be resized by dragging its edge.
A drag handle appears on the content-facing edge of the panel when enabled.
The resized width is persisted to localStorage.

types.name: PanelPosition
types.description: Which side of the screen the panel appears on.

types.name: PanelMode
types.description: How the panel interacts with page content: 'overlay' floats over it, 'push' shifts it aside.

pages.title: Theme
pages.description: Customize colors, dark mode, and fonts.

Update the theme at runtime.
Use this to sync with your app's theme (e.g., dark mode toggle).

properties.name: darkColors
properties.description: Custom color overrides for dark mode (when mode is 'auto' or 'dark')

properties.name: fontFamily
properties.default: "-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif"
properties.description: Font family for all panel text.

types.name: ThemeColors

Theme color configuration
All colors should be valid CSS color values (hex, rgb, hsl, etc.)

types.name: ThemeMode
types.description: Color mode for the panel theme. 'auto' follows the system preference.

pages.title: Context
pages.description: Set user context and manage identity.

methods.name: setContext
methods.description:
Set context for the assistant.
Use this to tell Pillar what the user is doing for smarter, more relevant assistance.

parameters.name: ctx
parameters.description: - Context fields to set (merges with existing context)

methods.name: getChatContext
methods.description:
Get the current chat context (conversation ID and messages).
Useful for escalation to human support with conversation history.

methods.name: identify
methods.description:
Identify the current user after login.

Call this when a user logs into your application to:
- Link their anonymous conversation history to their account
- Enable cross-device conversation history retrieval
- Associate future conversations with their user ID

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: - Your application's user ID for this user

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: - Optional user profile data (name, email, metadata)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: - Optional settings for the identify call

methods.name: logout
methods.description:
Clear the user's identity (logout).

Call this when a user logs out of your application.
Future conversations will be tracked anonymously until identify() is called again.

Note: This does not delete existing conversations - they remain associated
with the user's account for future retrieval.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: - Optional settings for the logout call

types.name: Context
types.description:
Context for the assistant.
Pass this to help the assistant understand what the user is doing.

properties.name: errorState
properties.description: Any error state the user is experiencing

types.name: UserProfile
Suggestion
types.description: Suggestion returned from the suggestions API.

pages.title: Cards
pages.description: Register custom card renderers for inline UI.

methods.name: registerCard
methods.description:
Register a custom card renderer for inline_ui type actions.

When the AI returns an action with action_type: 'inline_ui' and
a card_type in its data, the SDK will look for a registered renderer
and call it to render the inline UI card.

parameters.name: cardType
parameters.description: - The card type identifier (e.g., 'invite_members')

parameters.name: renderer
parameters.description: - Function that renders the card into a container

types.name: CardCallbacks
types.description: Callbacks provided to custom card renderers.

properties.name: onConfirm
properties.description:
Called when user confirms the action. Pass modified data if needed.
WARNING: Data passed here flows through the SDK pipeline (telemetry,
agent context, logs). Never include secrets, tokens, or PII.

properties.name: onCancel
properties.description: Called when user cancels the action

properties.name: onStateChange
properties.description: Called to report card state changes (for analytics/confirmation)

types.name: CardFieldSchema
types.description: Card field schema definition for declarative card configuration.

properties.name: validation

types.name: CardRegistrationOptions

properties.name: schema
properties.description: Schema defining form fields (for auto-generated forms)

types.name: CardRenderer
types.description:
Card renderer function signature.
Customers register these to render custom confirmation cards.
types.typeDefinition:
type CardRenderer = (
  container: HTMLElement,
  data: Record<string, unknown>,
  callbacks: CardCallbacks
) => (() => void) | void

pages.title: PillarConfig

properties.name: productKey
properties.description:
Your product key from the Pillar app.
Get it at app.trypillar.com

properties.name: platform
properties.description:
Platform identifier for code-first actions.
Used to filter actions by deployment platform.

properties.name: version
properties.description:
App version for code-first actions.
Used to match actions to the correct deployment version.

properties.name: debug
properties.description:
Enable debug mode for verbose logging and debug panel.
When enabled:
- All SDK events are logged to console
- A debug panel shows real-time execution flow
- Network requests/responses are captured

properties.name: edgeTrigger
properties.description: Edge trigger (sidebar tab that opens the panel).

properties.name: mobileTrigger
properties.description: Mobile trigger (floating button on small screens).

properties.name: urlParams
properties.description: URL params for auto-opening the panel.

properties.name: apiBaseUrl
properties.description: API base URL. Defaults to Pillar's production API.

properties.name: tracing
properties.description:
Enable OpenTelemetry tracing. Browser spans are exported to the server's
Cloud Trace project via the OTLP proxy endpoint. Also enabled when debug
is true.

properties.name: customCSS
properties.description:
Custom CSS to inject into the panel's Shadow DOM.
Use public class names (pillar-*) to override default styles.

properties.name: zIndex
properties.description:
Z-index configuration for the SDK root container.
Controls stacking order relative to host app content.

properties.name: onReady
properties.description: Called when the SDK is initialized and ready.

properties.name: onError
properties.description: Called when the SDK encounters an error.

subtypes.name: EdgeTriggerConfig

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Whether to show the edge trigger sidebar tab.
When enabled, a slim vertical tab appears on the screen edge that opens the panel.

properties.name: resizable
properties.description:
Whether the panel can be resized by dragging the edge of the sidebar trigger.
When enabled, a drag handle appears on the inner edge of the sidebar when the panel is open.
The resized width is persisted to localStorage.

subtypes.name: MobileTriggerConfig

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Whether to show the mobile floating button on small screens.
When enabled, a floating action button appears when viewport is below mobileBreakpoint.

properties.name: breakpoint
properties.description: Viewport width below which the edge trigger hides and mobile trigger shows.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: Preset icon to display in the button.

properties.name: customIcon
properties.description: Custom SVG icon string. Overrides the icon preset if provided.

properties.name: backgroundColor
properties.description:
Background color of the button (CSS value).
Defaults to the theme's primary color.

properties.name: size
properties.description:
Button size - preset name or pixel value.
- 'small': 44px
- 'medium': 56px (default)
- 'large': 68px

properties.name: label
properties.description: Tooltip text and aria-label for accessibility.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Offset from screen edges in pixels.

subtypes.name: MobileTriggerPosition
subtypes.description: Position of the mobile floating trigger button.

subtypes.name: MobileTriggerIcon
subtypes.description: Preset icon for the mobile floating trigger button.

subtypes.name: MobileTriggerSize
subtypes.description: Size preset for the mobile floating trigger button.

subtypes.name: UrlParamsConfig

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether to check URL params for auto-opening the panel (default: true)

properties.name: clearAfterOpen
properties.description: Whether to clear URL params after opening (default: true)

subtypes.name: InteractionHighlightConfig

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether to highlight elements when the AI interacts with them.

properties.name: outlineColor
properties.description: Outline color for highlighted elements (CSS color value).

properties.name: outlineOffset
properties.description: Outline offset in pixels (space between element and outline).

properties.name: duration
properties.description:
Duration in milliseconds to show the highlight.
Set to 0 for no auto-removal (highlight stays until next interaction).

properties.name: scrollIntoView
properties.description: Whether to scroll the element into view if not visible.

properties.name: scrollBehavior
properties.description: Scroll behavior when scrolling element into view.

subtypes.name: SuggestionsConfig

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Enable page-aware suggestion sorting.
When enabled, suggestions are re-sorted based on the current page context
whenever the user navigates to a new route.

properties.name: debounceMs
properties.description:
Debounce time for route change detection (ms).
Prevents excessive sorting on rapid navigation.

properties.name: displayLimit
properties.description: Maximum number of suggestions to display.

subtypes.name: SidebarTabConfig

pages.title: Other
pages.description: API items not yet assigned to a domain. These need to be categorized.

methods.name: getTools
methods.description:
Get all registered tools for debugging.
Returns tools from all sources: defineTool(), registerTool(), and onTask() handlers.

Only available when debug mode is enabled.

methods.name: executeToolForDebug
methods.signature: executeToolForDebug(toolName: string, input: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<{ success: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }>
methods.description:
Execute a tool by name for debugging purposes.
Only available when debug mode is enabled.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: - Name of the tool to execute

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: - Input parameters to pass to the tool

methods.returnType: Promise<{ success: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }>

methods.name: defineTool
methods.description:
Define a tool with co-located metadata and handler.

This is the recommended way to register tools. The metadata
(name, description, inputSchema) is discoverable by the CLI scanner
(`npx pillar-sync --scan ./src`) and the handler runs client-side.

If `execute` returns a value, it is automatically sent back to the
agent — no explicit `returns: true` flag needed.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: - Tool schema with metadata and execute handler

methods.name: registerTool
methods.signature: registerTool(tool: { name: string } & Record<string, unknown>): void
methods.description: Register a tool definition at runtime.

parameters.name: tool
parameters.description: - Tool definition with name and properties

methods.name: getRegisteredTool
methods.description: Get a registered tool definition by name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: - Tool name

methods.name: completeTool
methods.signature: completeTool(toolName: string, success?: boolean, data?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Signal that a tool has completed.

For simple tools, this emits the completion event.
For wizard tools (modals, multi-step flows), call this when the user
finishes the flow.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: - The tool identifier

parameters.name: success
parameters.description: - Whether the tool completed successfully (default: true)

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: - Optional result data

methods.name: sendToolResult
methods.signature: sendToolResult(toolName: string, result: unknown, toolCallId?: string): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Send tool result back to the agent.

Called automatically for tools with `returns: true` after their
handler completes. The result is sent to the agent for further reasoning.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: - The name of the tool that was executed

parameters.name: result
parameters.description: - The result data to send back to the agent

parameters.name: toolCallId
parameters.description: - Unique ID for this specific tool invocation (for result correlation)

methods.name: executeQueryTool
methods.signature: executeQueryTool(toolName: string, args?: Record<string, unknown>, schema?: { properties?: Record<string, unknown>; required?: string[] }): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Execute a query tool and send the result back to the agent.

This is called when the agent sends a `query_request` event.
Query tools are expected to return data that the agent can use
for further reasoning.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: - The name of the tool to execute

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: - Arguments for the tool

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: - Optional schema for parameter validation
